It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as anxiety, insomnia, and high blood pressure, due to its sedative and antihypertensive properties. The bioactive constituents of Ajuga reptans include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and glycosides. The most common herbal preparations made with Ajuga reptans include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
